notes: Coverage matrix assertion always passes with_skill (3/3) and always fails without_skill (0/3) — this is the most discriminating assertion and a clear skill benefit
Go table-driven test pattern fails for BOTH configurations — the skill does not specifically instruct Go idioms, improvement opportunity
Eval 2 (MessageParser) shows identical pass rate (75%) for both configurations but with different failures — skill adds matrix but misses payload verification
Skill adds ~73s average execution time and ~10.7k tokens per run for a +8.3% pass rate improvement
Anti-pattern assertions (no assert-does-not-throw) pass in 5/6 runs — one with_skill JUnit5 test had a weak assertNotNull
